general_provisions: This bill modifies requirements for social studies education and establishes open education resources.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>requires the state superintendent to identify and develop high-quality open educational resource instructional materials;<hr><ltbullet>requires social studies instruction on American self-governance and comparative government systems;<hr><ltbullet>requires instruction from documents influencing America's founding and literary thought;<hr><ltbullet>requires sourcing of English language arts assessment passages from primary source documents where appropriate;<hr><ltbullet>requires instruction on the roles and responsibilities of government officials;<hr><ltbullet>allows local education agencies to determine whether applied crafts and technical arts courses meet fine arts credit requirements;<hr><ltbullet>requires American constitutional government and citizenship to be taught as two distinct semester courses; <hr><ltbullet>requires United States history to be taught as two distinct semester courses; <hr><ltbullet>prohibits students from taking the same semester course twice to meet graduation requirements;<hr><ltbullet>requires literacy coaches to support the integration of social studies content into literacy instruction;<hr><ltbullet>requires regular social studies instruction for elementary grades;<hr><ltbullet>allows social studies instruction to be delivered through integrated instruction with English language arts and mathematics;<hr><ltbullet>requires implementation of elementary social studies requirements by July 1, 2031;<hr><ltbullet>repeals an adult education citizenship test and certain required plans;<hr><ltbullet>defines terms; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical changes.
